events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a reverse-lapped WRB (weather resistive barrier) where the upper layer is behind the lower layer. This is a clear defect - WRB must be installed with upper layers lapping over lower layers (shingle-style) to shed water. Reverse lapping will direct water behind the barrier, potentially causing water intrusion and damage.

This is a sequence violation in the flashing & WRB integration work. The work is in-progress, so it can be corrected before more work proceeds.

Key points:
- Reverse-lapped WRB is non-compliant with IRC R703 and manufacturer instructions
- This is an installed-defective condition
- Work must stop and the reverse lap must be corrected before continuing
- Risk is high because water intrusion can cause significant damage

Let me construct the JSON response.

  "rationale": "A reverse-lapped WRB is a fundamental sequence and installation defect. Water running down the wall will be directed behind the lower WRB layer, bypassing the drainage plane and risking moisture damage to sheathing, framing, and interior finishes. The defect must be corrected before any further work proceeds up the wall or over the affected area.",
